sections.title: Useful notes
items: "I **have to** finish this before tomorrow," he said.
Passengers **must** keep their bags with them at all times.

items.correct: You **mustn't** open that door! (= It is forbidden — do not do it.)
You **don't have to** open that door. (= You have a choice — it is not necessary.)

sections.title: Useful notes
items: His coat is here but he isn't. He **must** have stepped outside for a moment.

sections.title: Be careful
items: To speculate about past events, use a modal followed by the perfect infinitive (have + past participle).

exercises.exerciseTypeName: A: Complete with must, have to, need to or mustn't
exercises.title: A: Complete with must, have to, need to or mustn't
exercises.instructions: Write the best modal form in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write the best modal form in each gap.

items.sentence: You ...... show your ticket before entering the stadium.
items.explanation: Strong obligation/rule → **must** show your ticket.

items.sentence: We ...... hurry or the shop will close.
items.explanation: External necessity → **have to** hurry.

items.sentence: I ...... send this form before midnight.
items.explanation: Necessity → **need to** send this form.

items.sentence: Guests ...... take food into the swimming pool area.
items.explanation: Prohibition → **mustn't** take food in.

items.sentence: All players ...... wear the correct boots for the match.
items.explanation: Rule → **have to** wear correct boots.

items.sentence: You ...... open that file. It may contain a virus.
items.explanation: Prohibition/warning → **mustn't** open that file.

items.sentence: I ...... remember to lock the back door tonight.
items.explanation: Personal obligation → I **must** remember to lock.

items.sentence: Do we ...... arrive before eight?
items.explanation: Question about necessity → **have to** arrive before eight?

exercises.exerciseTypeName: B: Choose don't have to, don't need to or needn't
exercises.title: B: Choose don't have to, don't need to or needn't
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with a form showing no obligation.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each sentence with a form showing no obligation.

items.sentence: You ...... wash the dishes. I've already done them.
items.explanation: Not necessary → **don't need to** wash the dishes.

items.sentence: It's a bank holiday tomorrow, so Dad ...... go to work.
items.explanation: No obligation (bank holiday) → **doesn't have to** go to work.

items.sentence: You ...... come with us if you're too tired.
items.explanation: Optional → **needn't** come with us.

items.sentence: We ...... bring chairs; the school will provide them.
items.explanation: Not necessary → **don't need to** bring chairs.

items.sentence: Lily ...... study French this year because she chose German.
items.explanation: No obligation → **doesn't have to** study French.

items.sentence: You ...... answer now. Think about it first.
items.explanation: No rush → **needn't** answer now.

items.sentence: I ...... get a taxi because my brother can drive me.
items.explanation: Alternative available → **don't have to** get a taxi.

items.sentence: You ...... write your name twice on the form.
items.explanation: Unnecessary → **don't need to** write your name twice.

exercises.title: C: Past obligation
exercises.instructions: Complete using had to, didn't have to or didn't need to.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete using had to, didn't have to or didn't need to.

items.sentence: We ...... wait outside because the doors were locked.
items.explanation: Past obligation → **had to** wait outside.

items.sentence: I ...... take my umbrella because it didn't rain.
items.explanation: No past obligation → **didn't have to** take an umbrella.

items.sentence: Alex ...... finish his homework before dinner yesterday.
items.explanation: Past obligation → **had to** finish homework.

items.sentence: They ...... wear sports clothes because the match was cancelled.
items.explanation: Not necessary → **didn't need to** wear sports clothes.

items.sentence: I ...... ask for directions; I had a map.
items.explanation: Had a map → **didn't have to** ask for directions.

items.sentence: Clara ...... stay in hospital for two nights.
items.explanation: Past obligation → **had to** stay in hospital.

items.sentence: We ...... buy tickets. My uncle had already bought them.
items.explanation: Tickets already bought → **didn't need to** buy tickets.

items.sentence: Tom ...... get up early on Sunday, so he slept until ten.
items.explanation: No obligation on Sunday → **didn't have to** get up early.

exercises.title: D: Circle the correct phrase
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct option in each sentence.
exercises.studentInstruction: Choose the correct option in each sentence.

items.sentenceAfter: come if you have other plans.
items.originalSentence: You **mustn't / don't have to** come if you have other plans.
items.completedSentence: You don't have to come if you have other plans.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: There is no obligation — the action is optional, not forbidden.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Don't have to = no obligation. Mustn't = prohibition (not allowed).

wrongOptions.don't have to: Don't have to means optional — it does not mean forbidden.
wrongOptions.mustn't: Mustn't means something is forbidden, not merely optional.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Mustn't is one of the strongest ways to say something is not allowed.

items.sentenceAfter: touch the paintings in the gallery.
items.originalSentence: You **mustn't / don't have to** touch the paintings in the gallery.
items.completedSentence: You mustn't touch the paintings in the gallery.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The situation requires a strong prohibition or ban.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Don't have to = no obligation. Mustn't = prohibition (not allowed).

wrongOptions.don't have to: Don't have to means optional — it does not mean forbidden.
wrongOptions.mustn't: Mustn't means something is forbidden, not merely optional.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Mustn't is one of the strongest ways to say something is not allowed.

items.sentenceAfter: wait long because the bus arrived immediately.
items.originalSentence: We **had to / didn't have to** wait long because the bus arrived immediately.
items.completedSentence: We didn't have to wait long because the bus arrived immediately.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: No waiting was necessary — the action was not required.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Didn't have to = it was not necessary. Had to = it was necessary / obligatory.

wrongOptions.didn't have to: Didn't have to means unnecessary — not the same as had to.
wrongOptions.had to: Had to means obligation — not the same as no need to wait.

items.sentenceAfter: buy a birthday present for my sister.
items.originalSentence: I **need to / mustn't** buy a birthday present for my sister.
items.completedSentence: I need to buy a birthday present for my sister.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "need to" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "need to" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "mustn't" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.mustn't: "mustn't" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("buy a birthday present for my sister.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.sentenceAfter: wear a badge in this building.
items.originalSentence: All visitors **must / needn't** wear a badge in this building.
items.completedSentence: All visitors must wear a badge in this building.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "must" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "must" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "needn't" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.needn't: "needn't" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("wear a badge in this building.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.sentenceAfter: call a mechanic when her car stopped.
items.originalSentence: Nina **had to / didn't have to** call a mechanic when her car stopped.
items.completedSentence: Nina had to call a mechanic when her car stopped.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Waiting was necessary in this situation.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Didn't have to = it was not necessary. Had to = it was necessary / obligatory.

wrongOptions.didn't have to: Didn't have to means unnecessary — not the same as had to.
wrongOptions.had to: Had to means obligation — not the same as no need to wait.

items.originalSentence: You **needn't / mustn't** explain it again — I understand.
items.completedSentence: You needn't explain it again — I understand.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "needn't" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "needn't" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "mustn't" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.mustn't: "mustn't" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("explain it again — I understand.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.originalSentence: We **don't have to / mustn't** lose these documents.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The situation requires a strong prohibition or ban.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Don't have to = no obligation. Mustn't = prohibition (not allowed).

wrongOptions.don't have to: Don't have to means optional — it does not mean forbidden.
wrongOptions.mustn't: Mustn't means something is forbidden, not merely optional.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Mustn't is one of the strongest ways to say something is not allowed.

exercises.title: E: Probability and possibility
exercises.instructions: Complete with must, can't, couldn't, should, ought to, could, may or might.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete with must, can't, couldn't, should, ought to, could, may or might.

items.sentence: That ...... be Omar. He always wears that red jacket.
items.explanation: Logical certainty → **must** be Omar (red jacket).

items.sentence: This ...... be Anna's bag — she never carries a backpack.
items.explanation: Impossible → **can't** be Anna's bag.

items.sentence: The meeting ...... finish late if there are many questions.
items.explanation: Possible → meeting **might** finish late.

items.sentence: Your parcel ...... arrive tomorrow morning.
items.explanation: Expectation → parcel **should** arrive tomorrow.

items.sentence: I'm not sure where Dad is. He ...... be in the garage.
items.explanation: Possibility → he **could** be in the garage.

items.sentence: The shop is closed and the lights are off. It ...... be open.
items.explanation: Impossible (closed) → **couldn't** be open.

items.sentence: The team has trained hard, so they ...... win the match.
items.explanation: Expected result → **ought to** win the match.

items.sentence: Take a jumper — it ...... get cold later.
items.explanation: Possibility → it **may** get cold later.

exercises.title: F: Error correction
exercises.instructions: Each sentence has one modal verb mistake. Write the correct modal.
exercises.studentInstruction: Each sentence has one modal verb mistake. Write the correct modal.

items.sentence: You look ill. You must see a doctor.
items.explanation: Advice, not obligation → **should** see a doctor.

items.sentence: This should be Daniel's notebook. His name is on the cover.
items.explanation: Strong certainty → **must** be Daniel's notebook.

items.sentence: We must bring lunch; food is provided at the camp.
items.explanation: Food provided → **don't have to** bring lunch.

items.sentence: You needn't touch those chemicals. They are dangerous.
items.explanation: Dangerous → **mustn't** touch (not **needn't**).

items.sentence: She left an hour ago, so she can't arrive soon.
items.explanation: Left an hour ago → she **should** arrive soon.

items.sentence: He mustn't work late yesterday because the deadline was today.
items.explanation: Past obligation → **had to** work late yesterday.

exercises.title: G: Error correction
exercises.instructions: Each sentence has one modal error. Write the correct form.
exercises.studentInstruction: Each sentence has one modal error. Write the correct form.

items.displayPrompt: You don't have to cross the road when the light is red.

cues: You don't have to cross the road when the light is red.

items.explanation: Red light = prohibition → **mustn't** cross.

items.displayPrompt: I must to finish this exercise today.

cues: I must to finish this exercise today.

items.explanation: **Must** + base verb → **must finish** (no **to**).

items.displayPrompt: We had to not bring books to the trip.

cues: We had to not bring books to the trip.

items.explanation: No obligation → **didn't have to bring** books.

items.displayPrompt: That must be Sophie; she's on holiday in Portugal.

cues: That must be Sophie; she's on holiday in Portugal.

items.explanation: On holiday in Portugal → **can't** be Sophie.

items.displayPrompt: You needn't to pay for the museum today.

cues: You needn't to pay for the museum today.

items.explanation: **Needn't** + base verb → **needn't pay** (no **to**).

items.displayPrompt: This may is the correct password, but I'm not certain.

cues: This may is the correct password, but I'm not certain.

items.explanation: Possibility → **may be** the correct password.

exercises.title: H: Key word transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the second sentence using the word given.

items.sentence:
It's necessary for passengers to fasten their seatbelts. **MUST**
Passengers ...... their seatbelts.
items.explanation: Necessary → passengers **must fasten** seatbelts.

items.sentence:
It wasn't necessary for me to phone the office. **HAVE**
I ...... to phone the office.
items.explanation: Not necessary → I **didn't have** to phone.

items.sentence:
I'm almost sure that's Maya's umbrella. **MUST**
That ...... Maya's umbrella.
items.explanation: Certainty → **must be** Maya's umbrella.

items.sentence:
Maybe they'll visit us next weekend. **MIGHT**
They ...... us next weekend.
items.explanation: Possibility → they **might visit** us.

items.sentence:
It's prohibited to enter this area. **MUSTN'T**
You ...... this area.
items.explanation: Prohibited → **mustn't enter** this area.

items.sentence:
It's probable that the train will arrive soon. **SHOULD**
The train ...... soon.
items.explanation: Probable → train **should arrive** soon.

exercises.title: I: Mixed revision
exercises.instructions: Write one suitable modal form in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one suitable modal form in each gap.

items.sentence: You ...... bring a towel; the hotel provides one.
items.explanation: Hotel provides towels → **don't have to** bring one.

items.sentence: The film starts in ten minutes, so we ...... go now.
items.explanation: Urgent → we **have to** go now.

items.sentence: His phone is on the table, so he ...... be far away.
items.explanation: Phone on table → he **can't** be far away.

items.sentence: I ...... finish the poster today. The deadline is next week.
items.explanation: Deadline next week → **don't need to** finish today.

items.sentence: She practised a lot, so she ...... do well in the concert.
items.explanation: Lots of practice → she **should** do well.

items.sentence: You ...... feed the animals in the park. It's forbidden.
items.explanation: Forbidden → **mustn't** feed the animals.

items.sentence: The car is in the drive, so Dad ...... be home.
items.explanation: Car in drive → Dad **must** be home.

items.sentence: We ...... walk yesterday because Mum picked us up.
items.explanation: Mum picked us up → **didn't have to** walk.

items.sentence: I'm not sure, but this key ...... open the side door.
items.explanation: Uncertain → key **might** open the door.

items.sentence: The lift was broken, so we ...... use the stairs.
items.explanation: Lift broken → **had to** use the stairs.

exercises.title: J: Key word transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete the second sentence using a maximum of four words, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the second sentence using a maximum of four words, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence.

items.sentence:
There is no need for you to bring your dictionary.
...... bring your dictionary.
items.explanation: No need → **You don't need to** bring your dictionary.

items.sentence:
The rules say you cannot eat in this room.
You ...... in this room.
items.explanation: Rules forbid it → **mustn't eat** in this room.

items.sentence:
I'm certain he is in the garden now.
He ...... in the garden.
items.explanation: Certain → **must be** in the garden.

items.sentence:
It was necessary for every player to arrive early.
Every player ...... early.
items.explanation: Past obligation → **had to arrive** early.

items.sentence:
Perhaps she missed the bus.
She ...... the bus.
items.explanation: Possibility in past → **might have missed** the bus.

items.sentence:
You are not required to bring your own lunch.
...... bring your own lunch.
items.explanation: Not required → **You needn't** bring your own lunch.
